title: Aplilia pendula
taxonomy: Aplilia pendula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae, commonly known as the thyme-leaved family. It is native to South America and is often found in dry, sandy areas. Within the family Thymelaeaceae, Aplilia pendula is part of the genus Aplilia. This genus contains a small number of species, all of which are native to South America. Aplilia pendula is a shrub-like plant with small, white flowers.
